quickAnswer: NMU (Nelson Mandela University) registration for 2026 typically takes place in January (Semester 1) and July (Semester 2). Check the official NMU website at mandela.ac.za for exact 2026 registration dates. You can register online through the myNMU portal at mynmu.ac.za. Bring your ID, matric certificate, study offer letter, and proof of funding (NSFAS, bursary, or own funds) to in-person registration.
content:
NMU Registration Dates 2026 — Nelson Mandela University

Nelson Mandela University (NMU) is a comprehensive public university located in Gqeberha (Port Elizabeth), Eastern Cape, with additional campuses in George (Western Cape) and Missionvale. This guide covers everything you need to know about NMU registration dates for 2026, the online registration process via myNMU, required documents, and funding your studies.

NMU Registration Dates 2026

Registration PeriodApproximate Dates (2026)For
First-Year RegistrationMid-January 2026New students starting in Semester 1
Returning Student RegistrationJanuary 2026 (before classes)Continuing students for Semester 1
Late RegistrationLate January – early February 2026Students registering after the main period (late fee may apply)
Second Semester RegistrationJuly 2026New and returning students for Semester 2

⚠️ Important: NMU registration dates change annually. Always confirm exact dates on the official NMU website:

🔗 Check official NMU 2026 registration dates → mandela.ac.za

How to Register at NMU in 2026

Option 1: Online Registration via myNMU

NMU offers online registration through the myNMU student portal:

  1. Go to mynmu.ac.za — Log in with your NMU student number and password.
  2. Navigate to the registration module — Click "Register" or "Academic Registration".
  3. Select your modules — Choose the modules you are registering for, ensuring you meet all prerequisites.
  4. Confirm financial clearance — Your NSFAS, bursary, or own payment must be confirmed before you can complete registration.
  5. Submit your registration — Print or save your registration confirmation document.

🔗 Register online at myNMU → mynmu.ac.za

Option 2: In-Person Registration

First-year students and those who need assistance may register in person at their NMU campus. Walk-in registration venues include:

  • Summerstrand (South) Campus — Main campus in Gqeberha
  • Summerstrand (North) Campus — For Engineering, Science, and Built Environment
  • 2nd Avenue (South End) Campus — For Health Sciences and other faculties
  • George Campus — For students in the Western Cape
  • Missionvale Campus — Education and Technology students

NMU Registration Documents — What to Bring

DocumentNotes
Certified copy of ID / PassportCertified within the last 3 months
Certified matric certificate (NSC)First-years: certified copy of your official matric results
NMU study offer letterThe acceptance letter from NMU admissions
NSFAS approval letterIf funding your studies through NSFAS
Bursary confirmation letterIf using a bursary; shows NMU you can cover fees
Proof of payment / bank guaranteeIf self-funding; confirms you can pay tuition
Senior certificate / prior qualificationFor mature-age or transfer students

NMU Fees 2026

NMU tuition fees for 2026 vary by faculty and programme. As a public university, NMU fees are regulated and generally lower than private institutions:

FacultyApproximate Annual Tuition (2026)
HumanitiesR50,000 – R65,000
Business and Economic SciencesR55,000 – R70,000
LawR58,000 – R72,000
ScienceR60,000 – R75,000
Engineering, the Built Environment and TechnologyR65,000 – R85,000
Health SciencesR70,000 – R95,000
EducationR50,000 – R62,000
ArtsR52,000 – R65,000

Note: Fees are estimates. Check mandela.ac.za for the official 2026 NMU fee schedule.

NSFAS at NMU — Funding Your Studies

NMU is a public university and fully NSFAS-eligible. If you qualify for NSFAS, your tuition, accommodation, and living allowance costs can be covered. Key information:

  • Apply for NSFAS at nsfas.org.za before the annual NSFAS deadline (usually September–November of the prior year)
  • Your NSFAS approval must be confirmed before or at registration — NMU will not complete registration without financial clearance
  • NSFAS queries: Contact the NMU Financial Aid Office at +27 41 504 1111

Other Bursary Options for NMU Students

  • 📋 NSFAS — Primary funding for qualifying low-income students at NMU
  • 🏛️ Eastern Cape provincial bursaries — Government bursaries for EC students in education, nursing, agriculture, and social work
  • 💼 Corporate bursaries — Automotive, chemical, and manufacturing industries in the Eastern Cape fund NMU engineering and science students (e.g., Volkswagen, Isuzu, BASF)
  • 🎓 NMU merit bursaries — Available to high-achieving students; awarded by faculty offices
  • ⏰ 2026 deadlines — Most bursary applications close between April and June 2026

👉 Find 2026 bursaries for NMU students →

👉 Get 24/7 AI academic support from StudyBuddy →

NMU Contact Details

DepartmentContact
NMU Admissions+27 41 504 1111
NMU Emailadmissions@mandela.ac.za
NMU Student Portalmynmu.ac.za
NMU Main Websitemandela.ac.za
NMU Financial Aid+27 41 504 1111 (ask for Financial Aid)

⚠️ Disclaimer: StudyBuddy is an independent student support platform and is not officially affiliated with Nelson Mandela University (NMU). All NMU trademarks belong to their respective owners. Registration dates and fees provided are based on historical data and are subject to change — always verify at mandela.ac.za before taking action.

faqs.question: When does NMU registration open in 2026?
faqs.answer: NMU (Nelson Mandela University) registration for Semester 1 typically opens in mid-January 2026 for first-year students, with returning student registration also in January. Second Semester registration takes place in July. Always confirm exact dates on the NMU website at mandela.ac.za, as dates change annually.

faqs.question: How do I register at NMU online?
faqs.answer: NMU students can register online through the myNMU portal at mynmu.ac.za. Log in with your NMU student number and password, navigate to the registration module, select your modules, confirm financial clearance, and submit your registration. You must have NSFAS approval, a bursary letter, or proof of payment before you can complete registration.

faqs.question: What documents do I need for NMU registration?
faqs.answer: For NMU registration you need: a certified copy of your ID, a certified copy of your matric (NSC) certificate, your NMU study offer letter, and proof of funding — either an NSFAS approval letter, bursary confirmation letter, or proof of payment if self-funding. All certified documents must be certified within the last 3 months.

faqs.question: Does NMU accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes. Nelson Mandela University is a public university and fully NSFAS-eligible. NSFAS covers tuition, accommodation, and living allowances for qualifying students. Apply at nsfas.org.za and ensure your NSFAS approval is confirmed before NMU registration, as financial clearance is required to complete registration.

faqs.question: What are NMU tuition fees in 2026?
faqs.answer: NMU tuition fees for 2026 range from approximately R50,000 per year for Humanities and Education to R95,000 for Health Sciences and R85,000 for Engineering. Fees are set annually and vary by programme. Check the official NMU fee schedule at mandela.ac.za for exact figures.
